message:
fix(ft-nn): Unfold autograd via gather/pad/reshape; Fold rejects requires_grad inputs

Same severed-autograd pattern as the recent SELU / Threshold / Hardtanh
fixes: Unfold and Fold both read tensor_values, computed the
im2col / col2im in plain f64, and rebuilt a fresh leaf with
requires_grad=false — silently zeroing gradients flowing into upstream
parameters. The forward outputs were numerically correct, which is
why the existing forward-only tests didn't catch it.

Unfold (now fully differentiable):

  Compose through autograd primitives that already have backward
  kernels: pad → flatten → gather → reshape.

    1. tensor_pad along H, W with zeros so out-of-bounds kernel
       reads become legitimate reads from the padded buffer.
    2. tensor_reshape the padded input to 1-D.
    3. Pre-build a static gather index mapping each (batch, row,
       col_idx) output position to the flat padded-input index.
    4. tensor_gather along dim 0.
    5. tensor_reshape to [N, block_size, L].

  Gather's backward is exactly the scatter_add Fold would compute,
  which is the analytical transpose of Unfold — so the autograd graph
  is correct end-to-end.

Fold (forward-only with a loud guard):

  Composing Fold through tensor_scatter_add would require the
  ft-autograd ScatterAdd op to back-propagate through its `src`
  argument, which it currently does NOT (only the destination buffer
  receives gradient — see ft-autograd::backward where ScatterAdd's
  rule is explicitly "passthrough" for input only). Until that
  capability lands, attempting the composition would produce a graph
  that silently zeroes gradients into the input — exactly the bug
  this commit fixes.

  Bridge contract: when the caller passes a tensor that requires
  gradient, Fold returns a clear AutogradError explaining the
  limitation rather than silently corrupting training. Forward-only
  callers (the dominant existing use case, and what the existing
  fold_unfold_roundtrip test exercises) keep working bit-for-bit.

Tests:
  unfold_propagates_gradients: 3x3 input, 2x2 kernel — analytical
    derivative is the per-cell visit count under the kernel sweep
    (corners=1, edges=2, center=4 for stride=1). Pre-fix code
    returned None for the gradient.
  fold_rejects_requires_grad_input_loudly: confirms the Fold guard
    fails with the explanatory error for tracked inputs and still
    forwards correctly on detached inputs (so existing forward-only
    pipelines keep working).

The Fold autograd path can be unblocked in a follow-up by extending
ScatterAdd's backward kernel to gather the incoming gradient at the
index positions and accumulate to a tracked src node — at which
point Fold composes via zeros + reshape + scatter_add + narrow with
no further changes here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
